tools.name: search_members
tools.description: Search and filter members/accounts. Supports text search, date ranges, booking/activity history, membership tier, tags, and more. Use lastBookingBefore/inactiveDays to find inactive members.

tools.name: get_member_profile
tools.description: Get detailed profile for a specific member/account by ID.

tools.name: preview_customer_import
tools.description: Preview a bulk customer import (max 2000 rows). Matches existing accounts by phone/email and shows create vs update actions plus points/stamps/credit deltas. Use commit_customer_import with the returned sessionId to apply.

tools.name: commit_customer_import
tools.description: Commit a previewed customer import. Pass the same rows and sessionId returned by preview_customer_import. Creates/updates accounts and applies points, stamps, and credit deltas.

tools.name: create_account
tools.description: Create a CRM account/customer with a primary contact. Optionally enroll the account as a member; use enrol_membership later when the account already exists.

tools.name: update_account
tools.description: Update CRM account/customer profile fields such as name, phone, email, life stage, owner, notes, or member code.

tools.name: delete_account
tools.description: Soft-delete a CRM account/customer. This releases the primary phone for future reuse while keeping account history for audit.

tools.name: list_tags
tools.description: List all available tags for the merchant.

tools.name: create_tag
tools.description: Create a CRM tag for customer segmentation and organization.

tools.name: update_tag
tools.description: Rename or recolor an existing CRM tag.

tools.name: delete_tag
tools.description: Delete a CRM tag and remove it from all tagged entities.

tools.name: attach_tags
tools.description: Attach one or more tags to a member/account. Useful for segmenting members after filtering.

tools.name: detach_tags
tools.description: Detach one tag from one or more member/account records.

tools.name: list_tiers
tools.description: List all membership tiers with member counts and stats.

tools.name: get_membership_tier
tools.description: Get a specific membership tier by ID.

tools.name: issue_rewards
tools.description: Issue a gift/reward to multiple members. Requires a gift offer ID and member IDs.

tools.name: enrol_membership
tools.description: Enrol a member into a membership tier. Free tiers enrol immediately; paid tiers return PAYMENT_REQUIRED unless skipPriceCheck is set. Use list_tiers first to find the target tier.

tools.name: get_loyalty_balance
tools.description: Get a member's current loyalty balance — points, stamps, credits, lifetime totals, and active membership tier. Returns null if the member is not enrolled.

tools.name: earn_loyalty_points
tools.description: Credit loyalty points or stamps to a member. Use field="points" for points, "stamps" for stamps. Server records a transaction with the reason as audit context.

tools.name: get_dashboard_stats
tools.description: Get high-level business KPIs: member count, new members this week, active subscriptions, total points issued, active promotions, rewards redeemed, messages sent.

tools.name: list_bookings
tools.description: List bookings with filters for date range, status, service, staff, or member.

tools.name: get_booking_detail
tools.description: Get full booking details including line items, status history, and participants.

tools.name: get_booking_stats
tools.description: Get booking counts: today, this week, this month, and breakdown by status.

tools.name: get_available_slots
tools.description: Find available booking slots for a service on a specific date.

tools.name: list_services
tools.description: List booking services with name, price, duration, and status.

tools.name: get_service_detail
tools.description: Get full service details including addons, staff, resources, and tier pricing.

tools.name: create_service
tools.description: Create a bookable service. Sets duration, price, capacity, and policies. Use list_service_categories first if you want to attach to an existing category.

tools.name: update_service
tools.description: Update an existing booking service. Pass only the fields you want to change.

tools.name: create_service_schedule
tools.description: Create a bookable schedule window for a service, including recurring weekly/daily availability.

tools.name: list_service_schedules
tools.description: List schedule windows for booking services.

tools.name: update_service_schedule
tools.description: Update a service schedule window. Pass only fields to change.

tools.name: assign_staff_to_service
tools.description: Assign a staff member to a booking service. Pass users.id from list_staff.userId.

tools.name: link_resource_to_service
tools.description: Link a room/resource to a booking service.

tools.name: list_service_categories
tools.description: List service categories (groups for booking services like "Treatments", "Classes").

tools.name: create_service_category
tools.description: Create a service category (e.g. "Treatments", "Classes") to group bookable services.

tools.name: create_resource
tools.description: Create a bookable resource (room, equipment, vehicle). Used by services that have requiresResource=true.

tools.name: set_resource_availability
tools.description: Add an availability window for a resource. Use day-of-week + recurring=true for weekly schedules, or a specific date for one-off blocks.

tools.name: set_staff_availability
tools.description: Add an availability window for a staff member (companyMembers.id). Use day-of-week + recurring for weekly schedules, or a specific date for one-off changes.

tools.name: get_booking_settings
tools.description: Get global booking settings for the customer booking flow, cancellation rules, visibility options, and confirmation requirements.

tools.name: update_booking_settings
tools.description: Update global booking settings. Pass only the fields you want to change.

tools.name: list_tickets
tools.description: List support tickets with optional filters.

tools.name: get_ticket_stats
tools.description: Get ticket counts grouped by status.

tools.name: create_ticket
tools.description: Create a support ticket for a member issue.

tools.name: update_ticket_status
tools.description: Update the status of an existing support ticket.

tools.name: list_surveys
tools.description: List surveys with optional status/search filters.

tools.name: create_survey
tools.description: Create a survey with custom question blocks. Publish it later or set status to published for immediate portal access.

tools.name: get_survey
tools.description: Get survey detail, including blocks, publishing status, visibility, settings, and response count.

tools.name: update_survey
tools.description: Update a survey draft or publishing configuration. Use questionBlocks to replace the ordered form block list.

tools.name: archive_survey
tools.description: Archive a survey and remove it from active survey lists.

tools.name: get_survey_stats
tools.description: Get response and invitation metrics for a survey overview dashboard.

tools.name: get_survey_responses
tools.description: List responses for a survey.

tools.name: list_survey_invitations
tools.description: List survey invitation history without exposing token hashes or old invitation tokens.

tools.name: create_survey_invitation
tools.description: Create a token-auth survey invitation for a specific member/account or contact.

tools.name: list_survey_workflows
tools.description: List automation workflows attached to a survey.

tools.name: upsert_survey_workflow
tools.description: Create or update a survey automation workflow, usually triggered by survey_response_submitted.

tools.name: list_deals
tools.description: List CRM deals (sales pipeline) with optional filters.

tools.name: get_deal
tools.description: Get detailed CRM deal information by ID.

tools.name: create_deal
tools.description: Create a CRM sales pipeline deal, optionally linked to a customer account and contact.

tools.name: update_deal
tools.description: Update a CRM deal. Pass only fields to change; use null for nullable links/fields to clear them.

tools.name: delete_deal
tools.description: Soft-delete a CRM deal from the sales pipeline.

tools.name: update_deal_stage
tools.description: Move a deal to a different pipeline stage.

tools.name: mark_deal_won
tools.description: Mark a CRM deal as won and set close date.

tools.name: mark_deal_lost
tools.description: Mark a CRM deal as lost. Requires a lost reason.

tools.name: convert_lead
tools.description: Convert a lead account to an active customer. Optionally create a linked pipeline deal (Salesforce/HubSpot-style convert).

tools.name: list_contacts
tools.description: List contacts (people linked to CRM accounts), optionally filtered by account or search.

tools.name: get_contact
tools.description: Fetch a single contact by ID.

tools.name: create_contact
tools.description: Create a contact on an existing CRM account.

tools.name: update_contact
tools.description: Update fields on an existing contact.

tools.name: set_primary_contact
tools.description: Set a contact as the primary contact for its CRM account and demote other contacts on that account.

tools.name: delete_contact
tools.description: Delete a secondary contact. Primary contacts are protected; set another primary contact or delete the account instead.

tools.name: list_custom_fields
tools.description: List custom field definitions for CRM entities such as account, contact, deal, or booking.

tools.name: get_custom_field
tools.description: Get a custom field definition by ID.

tools.name: create_custom_field
tools.description: Create a custom field definition for CRM entities. Use options for select or multiselect fields.

tools.name: update_custom_field
tools.description: Update a custom field definition label, type, options, required flag, or sort order.

tools.name: delete_custom_field
tools.description: Delete a custom field definition and its stored values.

tools.name: get_custom_field_values
tools.description: Get custom field values for a CRM entity such as an account, contact, deal, or booking.

tools.name: set_custom_field_values
tools.description: Set or clear custom field values for a CRM entity. Values are stored as strings; pass null to clear a value.

tools.name: list_tasks
tools.description: List tasks with optional status, assignee, and account filters.

tools.name: get_task
tools.description: Get detailed task information by ID.

tools.name: create_task
tools.description: Create a task. Optionally link to a CRM account or assign to a team member.

tools.name: update_task
tools.description: Update a task (status, priority, assignee, due date, completion).

tools.name: delete_task
tools.description: Soft-delete a CRM task.

tools.name: search_crm
tools.description: Search across CRM accounts and contacts by name, email, or phone.

tools.name: list_invoices
tools.description: List invoices with optional filters. Use status "OVERDUE" to find unpaid invoices past due date.

tools.name: get_invoice
tools.description: Fetch a single invoice with line items, payment history, and customer detail.

tools.name: create_invoice
tools.description: Create a new invoice for a customer. Optionally include line items inline. Returns the new invoiceId.

tools.name: record_invoice_payment
tools.description: Record a manual payment against an invoice (cash, bank transfer, cheque, card terminal). Updates invoice status to PARTIAL or PAID based on the running total.

tools.name: mark_invoice_sent
tools.description: Mark an invoice as sent to the customer (transitions DRAFT → OPEN). Use after delivering the invoice via email or share link. Idempotent.

tools.name: void_invoice
tools.description: Void an invoice. Destructive — invoice can no longer be paid or edited; existing payments are preserved as history. Cannot void invoices that are already PAID or VOIDED.

tools.name: delete_invoice
tools.description: Delete a DRAFT invoice. Only invoices that have never been sent can be deleted; otherwise use void_invoice.

tools.name: restore_invoice
tools.description: Restore a previously deleted DRAFT invoice.

tools.name: get_invoice_stats
tools.description: Outstanding totals, overdue counts, and revenue this period. Used by the weekly-snapshot playbook.

tools.name: list_subscriptions
tools.description: List member subscriptions. Filter by status to find active, cancelled, or past-due subscriptions.

tools.name: get_subscription
tools.description: Fetch a single subscription with customer, tier, billing cycle, and renewal info.

tools.name: create_subscription
tools.description: Create a recurring subscription for a member. Bind to a membership tier or set a custom amount + cycle.

tools.name: pause_subscription
tools.description: Pause an active subscription (status → past_due, no further auto-renew). Use resume_subscription to reactivate.

tools.name: resume_subscription
tools.description: Resume a paused subscription back to active.

tools.name: cancel_subscription
tools.description: Cancel a subscription. Destructive — turns off auto-renew and prevents further billing cycles. Existing paid periods stay valid until end.

tools.name: list_campaigns
tools.description: List marketing campaigns (email, SMS, WhatsApp) with delivery stats.

tools.name: create_campaign
tools.description: Create a marketing campaign (email, SMS, or WhatsApp) in DRAFT. For email channelConfig, use subject + htmlBody + optional plainTextBody. recipientSource="list" is accepted as an alias for "individual".

tools.name: update_campaign
tools.description: Update an existing campaign. Channel cannot be changed after creation. Pass only fields to change.

tools.name: delete_campaign
tools.description: Delete a campaign. Only allowed in DRAFT status; sent campaigns are kept for audit history.

tools.name: restore_campaign
tools.description: Restore a previously deleted campaign.

tools.name: validate_campaign
tools.description: Validate a campaign before sending. Resolves contactable recipient count and previews the final email subject/body without sending.

tools.name: list_notification_logs
tools.description: List notification delivery logs for the current company. Recipients are masked by default for incident-response safety.

tools.name: list_conversations
tools.description: List messaging conversations across channels (WhatsApp, SMS, email, etc). Shows unread counts and last message time.

tools.name: create_broadcast
tools.description: Create a DRAFT WhatsApp or SMS broadcast campaign. The broadcast is NOT sent — admin must review and send it from the Broadcasts page.

tools.name: list_audit_logs
tools.description: List recent audit log entries for this company. Filter by action, resource, resourceId, user, source, outcome, destructive flag, and created date range.

tools.name: search_audit_logs
tools.description: Search audit logs by free text across action, resource, resource ID, changes JSON, and metadata JSON.

tools.name: get_audit_log
tools.description: Fetch a single audit log entry by ID, including before/after snapshots when available.

tools.name: revert_audit_action
tools.description: Revert an audited action by restoring the captured before snapshot. Only works for allowlisted target tables with full before/after audit snapshots.

tools.name: clone
tools.description: Duplicate an existing record (product, tier, campaign, post, service package, promotion, gift offer). Creates a copy in DRAFT status with "(Copy)" appended to the name.

tools.name: list_notes
tools.description: List notes attached to a member/account, booking, or ticket.

tools.name: create_note
tools.description: Add a note to a member/account, booking, ticket, or invoice.

tools.name: confirm_booking
tools.description: Confirm a pending booking.

tools.name: cancel_booking
tools.description: Cancel a booking. Optionally provide a reason.

tools.name: complete_booking
tools.description: Mark a booking as completed. Triggers commission calculation if configured.

tools.name: mark_no_show
tools.description: Mark a booking as no-show when the customer did not attend.

tools.name: list_promotions
tools.description: List promotion/coupon codes with optional status filter.

tools.name: get_promotion
tools.description: Get details of a specific promotion/coupon code.

tools.name: create_promotion
tools.description: Create a promotion / discount code. type=percentage or fixed_amount; value is the discount as a string ("10" = 10% or 10 currency units). Codes are uppercased server-side.

tools.name: update_promotion
tools.description: Update an existing promotion. Pass only the fields you want to change.

tools.name: delete_promotion
tools.description: Delete a promotion. Past usage records are preserved.

tools.name: restore_promotion
tools.description: Restore a previously deleted promotion.

tools.name: validate_promotion
tools.description: Check if a promotion code is valid for a given order. Returns isValid, discount amount, and an error code/message if invalid. Use before applying to a checkout.

tools.name: list_reward_schemes
tools.description: List loyalty reward schemes (points/stamps programs) configured for this business.

tools.name: list_staff
tools.description: List staff/team members in the business.

tools.name: list_resources
tools.description: List booking resources (rooms, equipment, etc.).

tools.name: list_service_packages
tools.description: List service packages/prepaid session bundles with optional search, status, sorting, and pagination.

tools.name: get_service_package
tools.description: Get service package detail including applicability and selected booking service/event items.

tools.name: create_service_package
tools.description: Create a service package/prepaid session bundle. Use applicableType=SELECTED with applicableItems to limit the package to specific booking services or events.

tools.name: update_service_package
tools.description: Update an existing service package. Pass only the fields you want to change.

tools.name: delete_service_package
tools.description: Delete a service package by ID.

tools.name: restore_service_package
tools.description: Restore a previously deleted service package by ID.

tools.name: get_plan_status
tools.description: Show the current organisation plan, subscription/payment state, enabled modules, and quota usage. Use before deciding whether an agentic operation is allowed.

tools.name: check_plan_operation
tools.description: Preflight whether an intended operation is allowed by token scopes, enabled modules, subscription state, and quota. Provide toolName, quotaCode, or moduleCode.

tools.name: list_plan_options
tools.description: List active FavCRM plans with prices, quotas, included modules, and whether each is the current plan.

tools.name: create_plan_upgrade_link
tools.description: Create a Stripe Checkout link for a new paid subscription, or a Stripe Billing Portal link for an existing Stripe subscription. Use only after the user explicitly asks to upgrade or fix billing.

tools.name: create_team_member_invite
tools.description: Invite a team member to this company and return a one-time MCP accept token. The invited user must verify an email code through accept_team_invite_request and accept_team_invite_verify before membership and API key issuance.

tools.name: accept_team_invite_request
tools.description: Step 1 of agentic team invite acceptance. Validates an invite token and sends a 6-digit verification code to the invited email. Then call accept_team_invite_verify with the code to join and receive an MCP API key.

tools.name: accept_team_invite_verify
tools.description: Step 2 of agentic team invite acceptance. Verifies the emailed code, joins the invited user to the company, and returns a one-time MCP API key for that user.

tools.name: get_whatsapp_connection_status
tools.description: Check whether WhatsApp Business is connected for this company, including WABA/phone metadata and the latest agentic connect session state.

tools.name: create_whatsapp_connect_link
tools.description: Create a short-lived browser link for WhatsApp Business Embedded Signup. The user must open the link because Meta requires a browser popup to authorize and choose the WABA/phone number.

tools.name: list_my_companies
tools.description: List all companies/organisations the current user has access to. Use this to see available orgs before switching.

tools.name: switch_company
tools.description: Switch the current session to a different company/organisation. Returns a new access token scoped to the target company. All subsequent tool calls will operate on the new company.

tools.name: list_posts
tools.description: List CMS posts (blog posts and/or pages) with optional filters.

tools.name: get_post
tools.description: Get detailed CMS post including content blocks, categories, and tags.

tools.name: create_post
tools.description: Create a new CMS post (blog_post, page, or any custom post type). The post type must already exist — use list_post_types to discover, create_post_type to add a new one. excerpt = plain-text summary only (auto-derived from blocks if omitted). Structured custom fields go in meta, keyed by the field sc

tools.name: update_post
tools.description: Update an existing CMS post (any post type). Only provided fields are changed.

tools.name: delete_post
tools.description: Delete a CMS post by ID. Soft-deleted posts can be restored with restore_post.

tools.name: restore_post
tools.description: Restore a previously deleted CMS post.

tools.name: list_image_models
tools.description: List AI image-generation models exposed to merchants (sanitized — provider/cost details hidden). Use to pick a `modelCode` for `generate_post_cover`.

tools.name: generate_image
tools.description: Generate an AI image via kie.ai and return its public R2 URL. Use to mint reusable URLs for content blocks (image, gallery), product photos, or any field that takes an image URL — does NOT attach to a post by itself. Costs credits (see list_image_models). On poll timeout the job continues running; p

tools.name: generate_post_cover
tools.description: Generate an AI image and attach it as a post's featuredImage. Submits a kie.ai job, polls until complete, copies the result into the canonical post-asset R2 folder, and writes the public URL onto the post. Costs credits (see list_image_models). On poll timeout, the job continues — call attach_post_c

tools.name: attach_post_cover_from_job
tools.description: Attach a previously generated ai-media job's output as the post's featuredImage. Use when generate_post_cover timed out (job kept running) or when reusing the same generation across multiple posts. Verifies the job belongs to your company and succeeded. Does NOT charge credits — credits were charged

tools.name: upload_post_cover_from_url
tools.description: Download an external image URL into R2 and attach it as the post's featuredImage. Replaces the manual flow of pasting external URLs (which break when the source goes down). Validates content-type starts with `image/` and rejects payloads larger than 20 MB. No AI credits charged — only standard stora

tools.name: list_categories
tools.description: List CMS post categories.

tools.name: create_category
tools.description: Create a CMS post category (e.g. "Equipment", "Injection — Botox"). For sub-categories, use namespaced names — the table is flat.

tools.name: update_category
tools.description: Update a CMS post category.

tools.name: delete_category
tools.description: Delete a CMS post category. Posts in the category are not deleted; they are simply unlinked.

tools.name: restore_category
tools.description: Restore a previously deleted CMS post category.

tools.name: list_post_types
tools.description: List all post types defined for this company (built-in: blog_post, page; plus any custom types).

tools.name: create_post_type
tools.description: Define a new custom post type (e.g. "treatment", "service"). Required before creating posts of that type. After creating a post type, use create_post_type_field to define its structured field schema. Those fields are stored in meta on each post — do not use excerpt for structured data.

tools.name: list_post_type_fields
tools.description: List the custom field schema for a post type. Use this before creating posts to know which keys to pass in meta and what types they expect.

tools.name: create_post_type_field
tools.description: Add a custom field to a post type schema. The field key becomes the key in meta when creating/updating posts. Use fieldType="repeater" with fields=[...] for repeatable structured rows. Use this instead of storing structured data in excerpt.

tools.name: update_post_type
tools.description: Update a custom post type. Built-in types (blog_post, page) cannot be modified.

tools.name: delete_post_type
tools.description: Delete a custom post type. Built-in types cannot be deleted. Fails if any posts of this type still exist — delete those first.

tools.name: restore_post_type
tools.description: Restore a previously deleted custom post type.

tools.name: update_post_type_field
tools.description: Update a field on a post type schema. Changing fieldType or flipping required=true is blocked when posts already use the field unless force=true is passed.

tools.name: delete_post_type_field
tools.description: Remove a field from a post type schema. Blocked when posts of this type still have data in the field unless force=true is passed (orphans the data).

tools.name: restore_post_type_field
tools.description: Restore a previously deleted field on a post type schema.

tools.name: reorder_post_type_fields
tools.description: Reorder fields on a post type. Pass field IDs in the desired display order; sortOrder is updated to match.

tools.name: append_post_block
tools.description: Append one or more blocks to the end of a post. Each block must be a valid block object: { id, version, type, data }. Types: paragraph, heading, image, list, quote, code, divider, spacer, embed, html, youtube, file, faq, callout, gallery, cta, accordion, product.

tools.name: replace_post_block
tools.description: Replace a single block at the given index (0-based). Avoids fetching the full post and re-sending all blocks via update_post.

tools.name: remove_post_block
tools.description: Remove the block at the given index (0-based). All subsequent blocks shift up by one.

tools.name: reorder_post_blocks
tools.description: Reorder blocks by passing block IDs in the desired order. The id list must contain every existing block id exactly once.

tools.name: create_booking
tools.description: Create a new booking for a member. Use get_available_slots first to find valid times, then create the booking with the service, member, date, and time.

tools.name: get_conversation_messages
tools.description: Get messages from a specific conversation thread. Shows the chat history between the merchant and a contact.

tools.name: find_or_create_workroom_thread
tools.description: Find or create a Workroom DM thread between a merchant user and an AI colleague. Use this when an inbound event deserves owner attention but no threadId was provided. This only prepares an internal Workroom channel.

tools.name: get_attachment_text
tools.description: Read the text/Markdown content of a file attached to a Workroom thread or sent by a customer (PDF, image, or document). Extracts on first read and caches the result. Returns status "ready" with markdown, or "skipped"/"failed"/"not_found" with a reason. Use when a message references an attachment you

tools.name: post_workroom_message
tools.description: Post an internal message from an AI colleague into a Workroom thread. Use this to report findings, ask the owner for approval, or share source links after reading CRM/Inbox data. This does not send anything to customers.

tools.name: create_inbox_reply_suggestion
tools.description: Create a draft reply suggestion that appears inline in the FavCRM Inbox composer. Use this for message.inbound events with replyPolicy="suggest"; it does not send anything to the customer.

tools.name: request_send_approval
tools.description: Create a merchant-owned Workroom approval request for a customer-facing send. The agent must not call send_message, send_whatsapp_message, send_test_campaign, or send_campaign directly.

tools.name: list_segments
tools.description: List customer segments. Segments group members by criteria for targeted campaigns.

tools.name: get_segment
tools.description: Get customer segment details including type, criteria, and live member count.

tools.name: get_segment_members
tools.description: Get the members that belong to a specific segment.

tools.name: create_segment
tools.description: Create a new customer segment. Use STATIC for manual member lists, DYNAMIC for criteria-based auto-filtering.

tools.name: update_segment
tools.description: Update an existing customer segment by id. Pass only the fields to change; criteria replaces the existing rules.

tools.name: preview_segment_count
tools.description: Preview how many customers would match dynamic segment criteria before creating or updating a segment.

tools.name: set_segment_members
tools.description: Replace the members of a STATIC customer segment with the given account IDs. The segment is converted to STATIC.

tools.name: delete_segment
tools.description: Delete a customer segment.

tools.name: list_templates
tools.description: List the company's WhatsApp message templates with their Meta approval status (PENDING / APPROVED / REJECTED). Check this to see which templates are approved before referencing one in a campaign.

tools.name: create_template
tools.description: Create and submit a WhatsApp message template to Meta for approval. Text templates only (header text, body with {{1}} variables, footer) — add buttons in the app. The template is NOT usable until Meta approves it (check with list_templates). Submitting consumes the store’s Meta template allowance.

tools.name: list_outlets
tools.description: List shop outlets/locations for the company.

tools.name: create_outlet
tools.description: Create a shop outlet/location. Optionally pass id to preserve a legacy UUID during migration.

tools.name: update_outlet
tools.description: Update an existing shop outlet/location by ID.

tools.name: list_products
tools.description: List shop products with optional filters by name, status, or category.

tools.name: get_product
tools.description: Get full product detail including images, options, and variations.

tools.name: create_product
tools.description: Create a new shop product as DRAFT. Merchant can review and activate from the portal.

tools.name: update_product
tools.description: Update an existing product. Only provided fields are changed.

tools.name: list_product_categories
tools.description: List all product categories for the shop.

tools.name: create_product_category
tools.description: Create a product category. Use parentId to create subcategories under an existing category.

tools.name: create_variation
tools.description: Add a variation (size, color, etc.) to an existing product.

tools.name: list_orders
tools.description: List shop orders with optional status and search filters.

tools.name: get_order
tools.description: Get full order detail including line items.

tools.name: update_order_status
tools.description: Update the status of a shop order (e.g. confirm, ship, cancel).

tools.name: list_offer_rules
tools.description: List upsell/cross-sell offer rules with optional filters.

tools.name: get_offer_rule
tools.description: Get offer rule detail including associated products and discounts.

tools.name: create_offer_rule
tools.description: Create an upsell/cross-sell offer rule as draft. Specify trigger type, conditions, and suggested products with optional discounts.

tools.name: query_favcrm_platform
tools.description: Search FavCRM MCP tools, enabled skills, and feature areas for how to use the platform. Use before guessing tool names or JSON arguments.

tools.name: search_tools
tools.description: Search the available FavCRM tool catalog for relevant operations. Use this when the curated direct tool list does not contain the operation you need.

tools.name: execute_tool
tools.description: Execute an available FavCRM tool by name after discovering it with search_tools. Target tool availability, input schema, module gates, and audit logging are still enforced.

tools.name: query_company_knowledge
tools.description: Search this company's reviewed knowledge base and return grounded snippets with source document IDs. Use for merchant policies, FAQs, pricing notes, brand guidance, and other uploaded references.

tools.name: list_knowledge_documents
tools.description: List merchant knowledge base documents (uploads + scraped URLs). Use reviewStatus/syncable to see what is ready for agent retrieval. Pass `updatedAfter` for delta sync. Reviewed content is fetched via GET /v6/merchant/ai/knowledge/{id}/content; source audit text is available with ?variant=extracted.

tools.name: get_knowledge_document
tools.description: Fetch a single knowledge document with metadata + extraction/review status. Reviewed content is served via GET /v6/merchant/ai/knowledge/{id}/content; raw/extracted variants are for audit.

tools.name: scrape_knowledge_url
tools.description: Fetch a URL's content into the knowledge base. Server crawls the URL, stores the response body in R2, returns the new document ID. Failures store the row with status='failed'. Use for adding marketing pages, FAQ docs, or external references the agent should be aware of.

tools.name: add_knowledge_text
tools.description: Add a free-form text document to the knowledge base. Use for pasted policies, FAQs, internal notes, brand voice references — anything the agent should be able to retrieve later. Stored as a single document; agent-side embedding/retrieval happens elsewhere.

tools.name: delete_knowledge_document
tools.description: Soft-delete a knowledge document. The agent loses access immediately, but the document can be restored with restore_knowledge_document.

tools.name: restore_knowledge_document
tools.description: Restore a previously deleted knowledge document.

tools.name: list_shipping_methods
tools.description: List all shipping methods configured for the shop.

tools.name: report_agent_issue
tools.description: Report a FavCRM platform issue when an agent finds a missing MCP path, tool failure, confusing schema, CLI/docs issue, or SDK fallback. Include logs, AI analysis, references, and clarification questions so the platform team can follow up.

tools.name: register_organisation_request
tools.description: Step 1 of agentic signup. Sends a 6-digit verification code to the email. After the user reads the code, call register_organisation_verify with it to finish and receive an API key. Use this when a user wants to create a new FavCRM workspace from inside an MCP client.

tools.name: register_organisation_verify
tools.description: Step 2 of agentic signup. Confirms the 6-digit code and creates the workspace. Returns an API key the agent should set as Authorization: Bearer <apiKey> for all subsequent FavCRM MCP calls in this session.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "get_attachment_text" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Read the text/Markdown content of a file attached to a Workroom thread or sent by a customer (PDF, image, or document). Extracts on first read and caches the re…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "register_organisation_verify" references sensitive artifacts (apikey, authorization, bearer). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Step 2 of agentic signup. Confirms the 6-digit code and creates the workspace. Returns an API key the agent should set as Authorization: Bearer <apiKey> for all…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_dashboard_stats, list_tickets, get_ticket_stats, search_crm, mark_invoice_sent, list_campaigns, create_campaign, list_notification_logs, list_conversations, list_notes, findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
